content: read reviews/531.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/531.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/531.md.

     2‚Üí
     3‚ÜíEvery item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
     4‚Üíwithout an explicit justification.
     5‚Üí

    11‚Üí- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.
    12‚Üí

    17‚Üí

    19‚Üí

    21‚Üí- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdŇĎs Problem 531`.
    22‚Üí- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/531`.
    23‚Üí- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (defines F(k) as the minimal N such that every 2-coloring of {1,‚Ä¶,N} contains a k-element subset with monochromatic non-empty subset sums; asks to estimate F(k)).
    24‚Üí- [x] Optional bibliography entries: references [ErSp89] (ErdŇĎs‚ÄďSpencer) and [BENTW17] (Balogh‚ÄďEberhard‚ÄďNarayanan‚ÄďTreglown‚ÄďWagner) with known lower bounds.
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí## 4. Namespace
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos531 ... end Erdos531`.
    29‚Üí- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos531`).
    30‚Üí- [x] `open Finset` and `open scoped BigOperators` appear **before** the namespace block (lines 36, 38).
    31‚Üí

    37‚Üí- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
    38‚Üí- [x] Prop uses `UpperCamelCase`: `HasMonoSubsetSumSet`.
    39‚Üí- [x] Function returning a value uses `lowerCamelCase`: `folkmanNumber`.
    40‚Üí- N/A: No `local notation` used (none needed).
    41‚Üí- N/A: No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks (optional; "consider" item).
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí## 6. Theorem Statements
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí- [x] Both `folkman_theorem` (line 63) and `erdos_531` (line 76) use the `theorem` keyword.
    46‚Üí- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_531` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
    47‚Üí- [x] `folkman_theorem` follows `snake_case` naming convention.
    48‚Üí- [x] Both theorems have `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5]`.
    49‚Üí- [x] Both theorems have docstrings: `folkman_theorem` (lines 59‚Äď61), `erdos_531` (lines 67‚Äď74).
    50‚Üí- [x] `erdos_531` docstring references [BENTW17]; `folkman_theorem` docstring mentions Sanders, Folkman, and Rado's theorem.

    56‚Üí- [x] Theorem type is a direct Prop (no `answer()` wrapper), consistent with Case 3.

    71‚Üí- [x] No duplicate numbers.

    85‚Üí- N/A: No variants using `erdos_531.variants.*` naming. The file has a single main conjecture plus one supporting theorem, each with its own attributes.
    86‚Üí
    87‚Üí## 11. Code Quality
    88‚Üí
    89‚Üí- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    90‚Üí- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `‚Ąē`, `‚ĄĚ`, `‚ąÄ`, `‚ąÉ`, `‚ąą`, `‚Č•`, `‚ąĎ`.
    91‚Üí- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    92‚Üí- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
    93‚Üí- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
    94‚Üí- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí## 12. Build Verification
    97‚Üí
    98‚Üí- [ ] `lake build` not run during this review. No obvious issues; expected `sorry` warnings only.
    99‚Üí- [ ] Custom linters not run during this review.
   100‚Üí
   101‚Üí---
   102‚Üí
   103‚Üí## Summary
   104‚Üí
   105‚Üí**Result: PASS** (all substantive items pass)
   106‚Üí
   107‚ÜíThe file is clean, well-structured, and fully conformant. Notes:
   108‚Üí
   109‚Üí1. **Supporting theorem** (¬ß10): `folkman_theorem` establishes the existence of F(k) (Folkman's theorem) and is appropriately included as a supporting result in the same file rather than as a formal variant.
   110‚Üí2. **AMS codes** (¬ß8): Only AMS 5 (combinatorics) is listed. One could argue AMS 11 (number theory) is also relevant given the coloring-of-integers setting, but 5 is the primary classification and the choice is reasonable.
   111‚Üí3. **Build verification** (¬ß12): Not performed during this review; should be confirmed in CI.
   112‚Üí

# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist ‚ÄĒ `deepmind/531.lean`

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdŇĎs Problem 531`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/531`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (defines F(k) as the minimal N such that every 2-coloring of {1,‚Ä¶,N} contains a k-element subset with monochromatic non-empty subset sums; asks to estimate F(k)).
- [x] Optional bibliography entries: references [ErSp89] (ErdŇĎs‚ÄďSpencer) and [BENTW17] (Balogh‚ÄďEberhard‚ÄďNarayanan‚ÄďTreglown‚ÄďWagner) with known lower bounds.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos531 ... end Erdos531`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos531`).
- [x] `open Finset` and `open scoped BigOperators` appear **before** the namespace block (lines 36, 38).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `HasMonoSubsetSumSet` (line 46) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 42‚Äď45).
- [x] `folkmanNumber` (line 56) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 53‚Äď55).
- [x] `folkmanNumber` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `sInf`, which involves classical choice).
- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
- [x] Prop uses `UpperCamelCase`: `HasMonoSubsetSumSet`.
- [x] Function returning a value uses `lowerCamelCase`: `folkmanNumber`.
- N/A: No `local notation` used (none needed).
- N/A: No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks (optional; "consider" item).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Both `folkman_theorem` (line 63) and `erdos_531` (line 76) use the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_531` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
- [x] `folkman_theorem` follows `snake_case` naming convention.
- [x] Both theorems have `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5]`.
- [x] Both theorems have docstrings: `folkman_theorem` (lines 59‚Äď61), `erdos_531` (lines 67‚Äď74).
- [x] `erdos_531` docstring references [BENTW17]; `folkman_theorem` docstring mentions Sanders, Folkman, and Rado's theorem.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem says "Estimate $F(k)$." The formalization captures the best known lower bound ($F(k) \geq 2^{2^{k-1}/k}$) as a direct assertion. This is **Case 3** ‚ÄĒ a direct assertion of a bound ‚ÄĒ so no `answer()` wrapper is needed.

- [x] Theorem type is a direct Prop (no `answer()` wrapper), consistent with Case 3.
- [x] No misplaced quantifiers as theorem parameters: `erdos_531` has no parameters; the `‚ąÉ k‚āÄ` and `‚ąÄ k` quantifiers are part of the Prop body.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] `folkman_theorem`: exactly one `@[category ...]` ‚ÄĒ `category research solved`.
- [x] `erdos_531`: exactly one `@[category ...]` ‚ÄĒ `category research solved`.
- [x] `research solved` is a valid category value (solved but not formally verified).
- [x] Both theorems contain `sorry` in their proof bodies (consistent with `research solved`).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Both theorems: exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute ‚ÄĒ `AMS 5`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (5).
- [x] Single number, so ascending order is trivially satisfied.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] 5 (combinatorics) is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] `folkman_theorem`: uses `by\n  sorry` format (multi-line, acceptable per checklist).
- [x] `erdos_531`: uses `by\n  sorry` format (multi-line, acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or `noncomputable def` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] `folkman_theorem` is a supporting theorem (Folkman's theorem ‚ÄĒ existence of F(k)), not a variant of `erdos_531`. It is appropriately named as a standalone result within the namespace.
- N/A: No variants using `erdos_531.variants.*` naming. The file has a single main conjecture plus one supporting theorem, each with its own attributes.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `‚Ąē`, `‚ĄĚ`, `‚ąÄ`, `‚ąÉ`, `‚ąą`, `‚Č•`, `‚ąĎ`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run during this review. No obvious issues; expected `sorry` warnings only.
- [ ] Custom linters not run during this review.

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (all substantive items pass)

The file is clean, well-structured, and fully conformant. Notes:

1. **Supporting theorem** (¬ß10): `folkman_theorem` establishes the existence of F(k) (Folkman's theorem) and is appropriately included as a supporting result in the same file rather than as a formal variant.
2. **AMS codes** (¬ß8): Only AMS 5 (combinatorics) is listed. One could argue AMS 11 (number theory) is also relevant given the coloring-of-integers setting, but 5 is the primary classification and the choice is reasonable.
3. **Build verification** (¬ß12): Not performed during this review; should be confirmed in CI.

1‚Üí/-
     2‚ÜíCopyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.
     3‚Üí
     4‚ÜíLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
     5‚Üíyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
     6‚ÜíYou may obtain a copy of the License at
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üí    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
     9‚Üí
    10‚ÜíUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
    11‚Üídistributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    12‚ÜíWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13‚ÜíSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 531
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/531](https://www.erdosproblems.com/531)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $F(k)$ be the minimal $N$ such that if we two-colour $\{1,\ldots,N\}$ there
    25‚Üíis a set $A$ of size $k$ such that all subset sums $\sum_{a\in S}a$
    26‚Üí(for $\emptyset\neq S\subseteq A$) are monochromatic. Estimate $F(k)$.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíThe existence of $F(k)$ was established by Sanders and Folkman, and also follows
    29‚Üífrom Rado's theorem. This is commonly known as Folkman's theorem.
    30‚Üí
    31‚ÜíKnown lower bounds:
    32‚Üí- ErdŇĎs‚ÄďSpencer [ErSp89]: $F(k) \geq 2^{ck^2/\log k}$ for some $c > 0$.
    33‚Üí- Balogh‚ÄďEberhard‚ÄďNarayanan‚ÄďTreglown‚ÄďWagner [BENTW17]: $F(k) \geq 2^{2^{k-1}/k}$.
    34‚Üí-/
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üíopen Finset
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üíopen scoped BigOperators
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üínamespace Erdos531
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí/-- A 2-coloring $\chi : \mathbb{N} \to \mathrm{Bool}$ admits a **monochromatic subset-sum
    43‚Üí$k$-set** within $\{1, \ldots, N\}$ if there is a $k$-element set
    44‚Üí$A \subseteq \{1, \ldots, N\}$ whose non-empty subset sums all lie in
    45‚Üí$\{1, \ldots, N\}$ and receive the same color. -/
    46‚Üídef HasMonoSubsetSumSet (Ōá : ‚Ąē ‚Üí Bool) (k N : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    47‚Üí  ‚ąÉ A : Finset ‚Ąē,
    48‚Üí    A.card = k ‚ąß
    49‚Üí    (‚ąÄ a ‚ąą A, 1 ‚Č§ a ‚ąß a ‚Č§ N) ‚ąß
    50‚Üí    (‚ąÄ S ‚ąą A.powerset, S.Nonempty ‚Üí ‚ąĎ i ‚ąą S, i ‚Č§ N) ‚ąß
    51‚Üí    ‚ąÉ c : Bool, ‚ąÄ S ‚ąą A.powerset, S.Nonempty ‚Üí Ōá (‚ąĎ i ‚ąą S, i) = c
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí/-- The **Folkman number** $F(k)$: the minimum $N$ such that every 2-coloring
    54‚Üíof $\{1, \ldots, N\}$ admits a $k$-element subset whose non-empty subset sums
    55‚Üíare monochromatic. -/
    56‚Üínoncomputable def folkmanNumber (k : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    57‚Üí  sInf {N : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÄ Ōá : ‚Ąē ‚Üí Bool, HasMonoSubsetSumSet Ōá k N}
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üí/-- Folkman's theorem: for every $k$, the Folkman number $F(k)$ is finite.
    60‚ÜíThat is, there exists $N$ such that every 2-coloring of $\{1, \ldots, N\}$ has a
    61‚Üímonochromatic subset-sum $k$-set. -/
    62‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    63‚Üítheorem folkman_theorem (k : ‚Ąē) :
    64‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ Ōá : ‚Ąē ‚Üí Bool, HasMonoSubsetSumSet Ōá k N := by
    65‚Üí  sorry
    66‚Üí
    67‚Üí/--
    68‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 531** (lower bound, [BENTW17]):
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí$F(k) \geq 2^{2^{k-1}/k}$ for all sufficiently large $k$.
    71‚Üí
    72‚ÜíThis is the best known lower bound on the Folkman number, due to
    73‚ÜíBalogh, Eberhard, Narayanan, Treglown, and Wagner.
    74‚Üí-/
    75‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    76‚Üítheorem erdos_531 :
    77‚Üí    ‚ąÉ k‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ k : ‚Ąē, k‚āÄ ‚Č§ k ‚Üí
    78‚Üí      (folkmanNumber k : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• (2 : ‚ĄĚ) ^ ((2 : ‚ĄĚ) ^ ((k : ‚ĄĚ) - 1) / (k : ‚ĄĚ)) := by
    79‚Üí  sorry
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üíend Erdos531
    82‚Üí

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 531

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/531](https://www.erdosproblems.com/531)

Let $F(k)$ be the minimal $N$ such that if we two-colour $\{1,\ldots,N\}$ there
is a set $A$ of size $k$ such that all subset sums $\sum_{a\in S}a$
(for $\emptyset\neq S\subseteq A$) are monochromatic. Estimate $F(k)$.

The existence of $F(k)$ was established by Sanders and Folkman, and also follows
from Rado's theorem. This is commonly known as Folkman's theorem.

Known lower bounds:
- ErdŇĎs‚ÄďSpencer [ErSp89]: $F(k) \geq 2^{ck^2/\log k}$ for some $c > 0$.
- Balogh‚ÄďEberhard‚ÄďNarayanan‚ÄďTreglown‚ÄďWagner [BENTW17]: $F(k) \geq 2^{2^{k-1}/k}$.
-/

open Finset

open scoped BigOperators

namespace Erdos531

/-- A 2-coloring $\chi : \mathbb{N} \to \mathrm{Bool}$ admits a **monochromatic subset-sum
$k$-set** within $\{1, \ldots, N\}$ if there is a $k$-element set
$A \subseteq \{1, \ldots, N\}$ whose non-empty subset sums all lie in
$\{1, \ldots, N\}$ and receive the same color. -/
def HasMonoSubsetSumSet (Ōá : ‚Ąē ‚Üí Bool) (k N : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÉ A : Finset ‚Ąē,
    A.card = k ‚ąß
    (‚ąÄ a ‚ąą A, 1 ‚Č§ a ‚ąß a ‚Č§ N) ‚ąß
    (‚ąÄ S ‚ąą A.powerset, S.Nonempty ‚Üí ‚ąĎ i ‚ąą S, i ‚Č§ N) ‚ąß
    ‚ąÉ c : Bool, ‚ąÄ S ‚ąą A.powerset, S.Nonempty ‚Üí Ōá (‚ąĎ i ‚ąą S, i) = c

/-- The **Folkman number** $F(k)$: the minimum $N$ such that every 2-coloring
of $\{1, \ldots, N\}$ admits a $k$-element subset whose non-empty subset sums
are monochromatic. -/
noncomputable def folkmanNumber (k : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
  sInf {N : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÄ Ōá : ‚Ąē ‚Üí Bool, HasMonoSubsetSumSet Ōá k N}

/-- Folkman's theorem: for every $k$, the Folkman number $F(k)$ is finite.
That is, there exists $N$ such that every 2-coloring of $\{1, \ldots, N\}$ has a
monochromatic subset-sum $k$-set. -/
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem folkman_theorem (k : ‚Ąē) :
    ‚ąÉ N : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ Ōá : ‚Ąē ‚Üí Bool, HasMonoSubsetSumSet Ōá k N := by
  sorry

/--
**ErdŇĎs Problem 531** (lower bound, [BENTW17]):

$F(k) \geq 2^{2^{k-1}/k}$ for all sufficiently large $k$.

This is the best known lower bound on the Folkman number, due to
Balogh, Eberhard, Narayanan, Treglown, and Wagner.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_531 :
    ‚ąÉ k‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ k : ‚Ąē, k‚āÄ ‚Č§ k ‚Üí
      (folkmanNumber k : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• (2 : ‚ĄĚ) ^ ((2 : ‚ĄĚ) ^ ((k : ‚ĄĚ) - 1) / (k : ‚ĄĚ)) := by
  sorry

end Erdos531

content.text: The review summary says **PASS**. No changes needed.
